title: Evangelion and Hermeticism
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion and Hermeticism.             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Jul 06, 2011 5:21 pm Well, after playing AC:B's new sequence, there was a few things I noted that was similar. Well, one specifically: A group of organized people call the Hermetics were trying get control of a "Number", and try to become gods by that. Well, that stirred something in my mind. After a little googling, I found this . I tried to read through it, but with my thick skull, I had a tough time understanding. Although, this was something I could understand: Once Hermeticism was no longer endorsed by the Christian Church it was driven underground and a number of Hermetic societies were formed. The Western esoteric tradition is now heavily steeped in Hermeticism. The work of such writers as Pico Della Mirandola, who attempted to reconcile Jewish Kabbalah and Christian mysticism, brought Hermeticism into a context more easily understood by Europeans in the Renaissance.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jul 06, 2011 6:23 pm Evangelion seems to have nothing to do with it.

[]:             Quote Post by skeithnal » Mon Jul 18, 2011 3:50 pm ^Sarcasm? hermetic references are freaking everywhere throughout evangelion! just one example (as I'm at work) the tree of the sephirot appears during instrumentality (and also in NME- the water refinement at the aquarium); the kabalah references and the overall secrecy and clandestine motions of all groups involved throughout the series reeks of hermetic traditions (groups withing groups with various levels of understanding poised to strike at each other). Read Foucault's Pendulum to see an extreme example (I dont know if it's just me, but I tend to draw a lot of parallels to concepts covered in this to NGE)

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Jul 19, 2011 5:42 am Evangelion appears to be steeped in Kaballah, rather than Hermeticism. These two mystic traditions have different origins, although parallels do exist. Note that I am no expert on either philosophy.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Jul 19, 2011 6:32 am Oh, absolutely. It is greatly steeped in the Kaballah, but the Hermetics also liked/spoke of the Kaballah in their studies.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Fri Jul 22, 2011 9:05 am Stryker wrote: Oh, absolutely. It is greatly steeped in the Kaballah, but the Hermetics also liked/spoke of the Kaballah in their studies.

Article: Evangelion and Hermeticism
[]: Evangelion and Hermeticism.             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Jul 06, 2011 5:21 pm Well, after playing AC:B's new sequence, there was a few things I noted that was similar. Well, one specifically: A group of organized people call the Hermetics were trying get control of a "Number", and try to become gods by that. Well, that stirred something in my mind. After a little googling, I found this . I tried to read through it, but with my thick skull, I had a tough time understanding. Although, this was something I could understand: Once Hermeticism was no longer endorsed by the Christian Church it was driven underground and a number of Hermetic societies were formed. The Western esoteric tradition is now heavily steeped in Hermeticism. The work of such writers as Pico Della Mirandola, who attempted to reconcile Jewish Kabbalah and Christian mysticism, brought Hermeticism into a context more easily understood by Europeans in the Renaissance.

Read Foucault's Pendulum to see an extreme example (I dont know if it's just me, but I tend to draw a lot of parallels to concepts covered in this to NGE)
